content.text: some change in current worktree, commit them

input.description: Show working tree status and diff

result.output:
On branch main

	.claude/
	.entire/

content.text: Only untracked files exist — no modified tracked files. Let me see what's in them before committing.

input.command: for d in .claude .entire log; do echo "=== $d ==="; git ls-files --others --exclude-standard "$d" | head -50; done
input.description: List untracked files in each directory

input.command: echo "=== .claude/settings.json ==="; cat .claude/settings.json; echo; echo "=== .entire/settings.json ==="; cat .entire/settings.json; echo; echo "=== .entire/.gitignore ==="; cat .entire/.gitignore; echo; echo "=== log sizes ==="; ls -la log/ .claude/log/

content.text: These are all local tooling artifacts (from the `entire` CLI and Claude Code). Notably, `log/` and `.claude/log/` are **empty transient log files** — those don't belong in the repo. The meaningful, committable changes are the config files. Let me commit those and leave the logs out.

input.description: Stage config files, excluding log dirs

result.output:
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.

	new file:   .entire/.gitignore
	new file:   .entire/settings.json

Untracked files:

input.command: git commit -m "Add entire CLI and Claude Code tooling config"
